fact: Ed's first job after college was a Software Engineer for IBM in the UK
Ed spent the majority of his career at JPMorgan Chase, first as a developer, then manager, then executive
Ed's favorite fruit is a banana
Ed's favorite musician for a live performance is Karol G
Ed studied Physics at the University of Oxford from 1992 to 1995
Ed is the Co-Founder and CTO of Nebula.io, an AI-powered talent recruitment platform
Ed founded untapt in October 2013, an AI startup that matches candidates with jobs using deep learning
untapt was acquired in 2021 by Wynden Stark Group, the parent company of GQR, and became Nebula.io
Ed reached the level of Managing Director at JPMorgan, leading a global team of 300 developers
At JPMorgan, Ed worked in London, Tokyo and New York over 11 years
Ed is based in New York, New York
Ed's top skills include LLMs, Agents and speaking about them
Ed is the author of best-selling Udemy courses on AI Engineering and Agents with over 500,000 students enrolled
Ed's Udemy courses have reached students in 194 countries
Ed speaks at O'Reilly Live Events and ODSC workshops on AI and Agents
Ed holds a patent titled 'Apparatus for determining role fitness while eliminating unwanted bias'
Nebula.io uses proprietary LLMs to help recruiters source, understand, engage and manage talent
Ed is an AI Advisor at Simplified.Travel, helping with personalized itinerary solutions
Ed learned to code at age 8 and still spends weekends experimenting with LLMs
Ed is an amateur electronic music producer and was once an amateur DJ
Ed is a regular reader of Hacker News
At IBM, Ed taught classes on Smalltalk and Object Technology across Europe
untapt was selected for the Accenture FinTech Innovation Lab accelerator program
untapt was named an American Banker Top 20 Company To Watch
Ed's startup was voted 'startup most likely to grow exponentially' at an Amazon pitch event
Ed has been featured in Fast Company, Forbes, and American Banker publications
Ed was interviewed on the floors of NYSE and Nasdaq about untapt
Nebula.io's long-term mission is inspired by Ikigai, helping people discover their potential and pursue fulfillment
Ed co-presented on Agents at ODSC Boston, a talk that received over 280,000 views
Ed's Udemy courses include 'AI Coder: Vibe Coder to Agentic Engineer' and 'AI Builder with n8n'
At JPMorgan, Ed built Python simulation engines that calculate counterparty credit risk for the Derivatives portfolio
Ed can be reached via email at ed@edwarddonner.com or on Twitter/X at @edwarddonner or YouTube at youtube.com/@edward.donner
Ed likes to connect on LinkedIn at https://www.linkedin.com/in/eddonner/
Ed goes by Ed or Edward, whichever you prefer
Ed describes himself as a technology leader, speaker and entrepreneur, but at his core a software engineer and scientist
Ed worked at Cygnifi, a JPMorgan FinTech spin-off, as a project leader building Interest Rate Derivatives platforms
